frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment:
A: Kal Sharma ji ki beti ko dekha, usually jo western outfits mein rehti hai, par function mein saree pehni thi.
B: Haan, tabhi toh bilkul graceful aur decent lag rahi thi. Sach kahun, hamari Indian women saree ya salwar mein hi apni asli personality dikhati hain; baaki kapdon mein phir log galat message le lete hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's respectability and 'grace' to traditional clothing while implying non-traditional clothes send a 'wrong message.' It encodes the surface manifestations of praising traditional wear as superior and subtly blaming harassment or judgment on women's clothing choices.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
